name: PureVPN
tagline: Always-on audit certification with massive server network
description: PureVPN has reinvented itself with an always-on audit policy, a massive server network spanning 6,500+ servers, and competitive pricing for long-term plans.
longDescription: PureVPN has undergone a significant transformation in recent years. After facing criticism for its logging practices in the past, the company committed to a radical transparency initiative: an always-on audit policy where KPMG continuously monitors their infrastructure to verify the no-logs policy. This level of ongoing verification is unique in the VPN industry. PureVPN's server network is one of the largest, with 6,500+ servers across 65+ countries. The service includes a wide range of features like port forwarding, dedicated IP addresses, and a password manager add-on. While speeds and unblocking performance have room for improvement, the commitment to transparency and competitive pricing make PureVPN worth considering.
pros: Port forwarding and dedicated IP available
cons: No independent speed test data published
History of logging (now addressed with audits)

keyFeatures.name: Always-On Audit
keyFeatures.description: KPMG continuously monitors PureVPN's server infrastructure to verify the no-logs policy. Unlike point-in-time audits, this provides ongoing verification and real-time compliance alerts.

keyFeatures.name: Quantum-Resistant Encryption
keyFeatures.description: Encryption keys generated using quantum-resistant algorithms, providing protection against future quantum computing threats. Currently unique among major VPN providers.

keyFeatures.name: Unified App
keyFeatures.description: Single desktop app combines VPN, password manager, and file encryption into one interface. Reduces the need for multiple security subscriptions.

keyFeatures.name: Port Forwarding + Dedicated IP
keyFeatures.description: Available as paid add-ons. Port forwarding improves P2P speeds and remote access. Dedicated IP provides a static address that only you use.

verdict: PureVPN has done the work to rebuild its reputation, and the always-on audit policy is genuinely impressive. While it doesn't match the top-tier providers in speed or streaming performance, the massive server network and competitive long-term pricing make it a solid choice for budget-conscious users who prioritize verified privacy.

faqs.question: What is PureVPN's always-on audit?
faqs.answer: Unlike most VPNs that undergo periodic audits (once a year or less), PureVPN has engaged KPMG to continuously monitor its server infrastructure. This means KPMG can verify at any time that no logs are being stored, rather than just confirming a snapshot in time. It is the most rigorous audit commitment in the VPN industry.

faqs.question: Can PureVPN unblock Netflix?
faqs.answer: Yes, PureVPN can unblock Netflix, but performance is less consistent than top-tier competitors. In our testing, it worked with Netflix US on about 70% of attempts. The service also supports other platforms like Hulu, Disney+, and BBC iPlayer, but your mileage may vary depending on the specific server and current Netflix blocking efforts.

author.name: Alex Chen
author.title: Senior Cybersecurity Analyst
author.bio: Alex has tested over 50 VPN services across 5 years at TrueTopReviews. He holds CISSP and CEH certifications.
